def: s.m. = ni-mish, q.v. s.m. Winking ortwinkling (of the eye); — a twinkling (of the eye, considered as a measure of time), a moment, an instant.
s. m. A moment (of time), a twinkling of the eye.
s.m. corr. of nimish, q.v. s.m. Winking ortwinkling (of the eye); — a twinkling (of the eye, considered as a measure of time), a moment, an instant.
s.m. Shutting the eyes, &c. (i.q. nimīlikā); — closing the eyes in death; dying, death.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Closing the eyes; having the eye-lids shut; — one who has the eyes shut.
s.f. Shutting the eyes, winking; twinkling of the eye; blinking; conniving (at).
s.m. Winking ortwinkling (of the eye); — a twinkling (of the eye, considered as a measure of time), a moment, an instant.
s.m. = naimishāraṇya, q.v.s.v. naimish.

ety: S. निमित्तं; and निमित्ते, loc. of nimitta
def: s.m. A mark, an aim; — a sign, token; — a prognostic, presage, omen; — cause, motive, reason, instrumental or efficient cause; — (met.) share, allotment; fortune; — (S. nimitte) postpn. Because (of, -ke), by reason (of); for the benefit (of); for the purpose (of), through; for the sake (of); for; on account (of); with a view (to)
more: n. m. 1. Cause; motive. 2. Share; allotment. is nimant se , H. For the sake of; on account of.
s. m. Cause, motive, instrument; for the sake. ( met. ) Fortune. نمتي nimitt-ī, or نمتمان nimitt-mān, adj. Prosperous.
subs: s.m. An instrumental or efficient cause (esp. the Deity considered as the agent in creation)
Because of this; for the sake of this; on account of this
For that; instead of that; for him, &c.
For his sake; in his interest

def: s.m. See s.v. nimitt. s.m. A mark, an aim; — a sign, token; — a prognostic, presage, omen; — cause, motive, reason, instrumental or efficient cause; — (met.) share, allotment; fortune; — (S. nimitte) postpn. Because (of, -ke), by reason (of); for the benefit (of); for the purpose (of), through; for the sake (of); for; on account (of); with a view (to)
subs: s.m. An instrumental or efficient cause (esp. the Deity considered as the agent in creation)
Because of this; for the sake of this; on account of this
For that; instead of that; for him, &c.
For his sake; in his interest

def: s.f. = S نمتتتتو निमित्तत्व nimittatva, s.m. The state of being a cause, causality, instrumentality.
s.m. = S نمتتتا निमित्तता nimittatā, s.f. The state of being a cause, causality, instrumentality.
adj. Fortunate, prosperous, possessed of means.
s.f. = nimb-kauṛī, q.v.

ety: nimak, for namak, q.v.+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) Pickled limes.
s.m. Setting of the sun, sunset.
s.m. Depth, low ground, lowland; — a chasm or cavity (in the ground); a depression; — slope, declivity; — adj. (f. -ā), Deep, profound; low (as ground); — depressed; sunk; — stooping
subs: adj. (f. -ā), Going low or deep; going downwards, descending

ety: niman+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Strength, power, vigour; stoutness; tightness.
s. f. Strength, stoutness.

ety: nimnā˚ = niman, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे or आव = S. आपि (caus. aug.) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To render strong, or tight, or good; to strengthen; — to ameliorate.
more: v. a. To strengthen, to ameliorate.

def: s.f. Deepness, depth, lowness, profundity.
postpos. Like: see نمن naman, or مانند mānand.
v. a. See bhugtānā , v. a.
s.m. (dialec. or rustic) = nibṭāʼo, q.v. s.m. Determination, settlement, &c. (i.q. nipṭāʼo, and nibṭerā, qq.v.).
n. m. ( Brokers ) Eight annas ( ṭālī ).
s.m. = nibṭerā, q.v. s.m. Settlement, adjustment, determination, decision, discharge; accomplishment, completion.
s.m. (dialec.) = nimbū, or nīṅbū, q.v. s.m. The common lime, Citrus acida, or its fruit (also written nīṅbū, nīmū, and līmū).

ety: Prk. निम्मसूओ, or निम्मंछूओ; S. निःश्मश्रु+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Without whiskers or moustaches; — a man without moustaches; — a youth.
more: n. m. 1. A man without moustaches. 2. A youth ( gabrū ).

def: adj. & s.m. = نمہا ni-muhā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), 'Having no mouth'; voiceless; speechless, silent, dumb, quiet; — meek; — unmindful of what he has said or promised; — a silent, or quiet person, &c.; — one who pays no heed to what he has said, one who does not keep his promise.

adj. 1. Silent; quiet. 2. Meek. Du-mūhī rassī ḍase unke donoṅ hāthoṅ ko, Ni-mūhī jān-ke woh mujh ko mār lete haiṅ . Wom. Jān Sāhab. By serpent double-tongued may both his hands stung be! Knowing me helpless, meek he hath afflicted me.

adv. (dialec.) A suffixed emphatic particle (i.q. nī, q.v.). A negative or privative prefix (used euphonically for, or corrupted from, nir, nis, or nih̥), e.g. ni-ḍar, 'Fearless' (numerous instances of the use of this prefix will be found in the following pages).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit नयन (nayana), from the root नी (nī), from Proto-Indo-European *neyH-.

def: s.m. Sound, noise, cry; buzz, hum (i.q. ni-nad).
s.m. Sound, noise, cry; humming, buzzing (i.q. ninād).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi نند (nand /⁠nanad⁠/) / ننند (nanad), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀡𑀁𑀤𑀸 (ṇaṇaṃdā), from Sanskrit ननान्दृ (nánāndṛ), ननन्दृ (nanandṛ).

def: adj. (f. -inī), Sounding, resounding, ringing; — causing to sound, playing (as a musical instrument); — accompanied by the sound (of).
adj. = ninānwe, q.v.
adj. Ninety-nine
more: adj. Ninety nine. ننانوي کي پهير مين پڙنا ninānawe ke pher meṅ paṛnā, 1. To be entirely absorbed in the acquisition of wealth. 2. To be involved in difficulties.
subs: 'To be deep in the calculation of the ninety and nine'; to be entirely absorbed in the acquisition of wealth; — to be involved in difficulties.

ety: Ap. Prk. निनावँउ; Prk. निन्नामओ; S. निस्+नाम+कः
def: s.m. lit. 'The nameless one' (i.e. not to be named); — anything which it is unlucky to name (esp. cholera); — the thrush, aphthœ.

ety: Ap. Prk. निनावँउ; Prk. निन्नामओ; S. निस्+नाम+कः
def: s.m. lit. 'The nameless one' (i.e. not to be named); — anything which it is unlucky to name (esp. cholera); — the thrush, aphthœ.
adj. = vi-nāśī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Being destroyed, perishing; undergoing change or transformation; — destroying, destructive; — perishable, destructible; — a destroyer, &c. (i.q. vināśak).
s.m. = nināṅwāṅ, q.v. s.m. lit. 'The nameless one' (i.e. not to be named); — anything which it is unlucky to name (esp. cholera); — the thrush, aphthœ.
n. m. H. ni + nām , nameless. 1. Something which it is unlucky to name, esp. Cholera. 2. Thrush in the mouth.
s.m. The thrush, aphthœ; — a bug.
s.m. Carrying out, performing; accomplishment; performance (of a ceremony, &c.).
s.m. The nīm-tree (see nīm).
s.f. The fruit or berry of the Nīm tree.
s.m. (dialec.) = nimbū, q.v. s.m. The common lime, Citrus acida, or its fruit (also written nīṅbū, nīmū, and līmū).
more: s. (dakh.) Lemon, lime ( Harris ): see ليمو līmū.

def: s.f. Sleep
more: n. f. S. निद्रा Pālī, niddā , Pr. neddā . Sleep. 1. Sūlī par bhī nīṅd ātī hai . Prov. Saying. Sleep comes even to the impaled. 2. Kāgā! mat bol du-pahrī māṅ! terā bol bandhā lāve sai mere nīṅd ke lahre māṅ . Rus. Dont caw at midday, crow! it breaks my sleep, you know. nīṅd uchaṭnā, nīṅd uchāṭ h , v. n. To have one's sleep broken. Jo sote meṅ ā jāë uskī lapaṭ Phaṛak jāë dil, nīṅd jāë uchaṭ. nīṅd ānā , v. n. To feel sleepy. Kaise āve nīṅd, nanadyā, paurī meṅ āsak ṭhāḍo! Rus. Wom. song. How can I sleep, my sister-in-law, my lover's waiting at the gate! nīṅd bhar-ke sonā , v. n. To have one's fill of sleep. nīṅd harām k. , v. a. To disturb one's sleep. nīṅd meṅ h. , v. n. To be sleepy. nīṅdā'sā, niṅdail, nidrālū , adj. Pr. ṅiddālu , Sleepy; dozing.
s. f. Sleep. نيند اچاٿ هونا nīnd uchāṭ honā, To be unable to sleep, or to have sleep broken. نيند بهرسونا nīnd bhar sonā, To sleep sound, to be at ease.
subs: or nīṅd ućāṭ honā, v.n. Sleep to be dissipated, or to vanish; to have one's sleep broken; to be unable to sleep
(-ko), 'Sleep to visit the eyes,' to feel sleepy
adj. Having had abundance of sleep
or nīṅd-bhar-ke sonā, v.n. To sleep soundly; to have one's fill of sleep; to be at ease
ex: ہم سو نہیں رہے ہیں۔ ہم سو کیسے سکتے ہیں ،اُس بوڑھے نے تو ہماری نیندیں اُڑا دی ہیں۔
ham so nahī̃ rahe ha͠i. ham so kaise sakte ha͠i, us būṛhe ne to hamārī nīndẽ uṛā dī ha͠i.
We're not sleeping. How can we sleep? That old man has crashed our sleep.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi نیند (nīnd) / نید (nīd), from Old Hindi نیند (nīnd /⁠nynd⁠/) / نید (nīd /⁠nyd⁠/) / نندا (nindā), from Prakrit 𑀡𑀺𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀸 (ṇiddā), from Sanskrit निद्रा (nidrā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *nidráH.

def: s.f. Blame, censure, reproach, reproof; — abuse; insult; reviling; scorn; — evil-speaking, disparagement, defamation, slander; blasphemy
more: n. f. S. णिद Evil-speaking ( bad-goī ). nindā-patr, nindā-lekh , n. m. A libel. nindā k., nindnā, nindrā k. , v. n. To speak ill of; backbite. 1. Sādhan khāī, santan khāī, khāī Kuvar Kanhāī , Bacchanals. Jo bijyā kī nindā kareṅ, use khāë Kālikā māī! Saints pure and holy men do drink, and so doth prince Kanhaë, May mother Kalka him devour who dares our bhang decry! 2. Gurū kī nindrā nahīṅ karte . One should not speak ill of his preceptor. nind'at , adj. Slandered; calumniated. nind'ak , n. m. A slanderer; calumniator.
s. f. Censure, reproach, scorn, defamation.
subs: or nindā-lekh, s.m. Defamatory or libellous writing, a libel
(-kī), To revile; to scorn; to abuse, insult; — to speak ill (of), disparage, slander, &c.; to blaspheme.

def: adj. Sleepy, drowsy (i.q. niṅdāsā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Sleepy; drowsy.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Blaming, reproaching, reproachful; defaming; abusive, scurrilous; — censorious, querulous; — blamer, reprover; — defamer, slanderer, calumniator; — scorner; — blasphemer.
more: s. m. A calumniator, scorner. adj. Censorious, querulous.

ety: nindak, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Blaming; — defamation; — censoriousness, querulousness.
s. f. Querulousness, censoriousness.

adj. & s.m. = nindak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Blaming, reproaching, reproachful; defaming; abusive, scurrilous; — censorious, querulous; — blamer, reprover; — defamer, slanderer, calumniator; — scorner; — blasphemer.
s.m. Blame, censure, reproach (i.q. nindā, q.v.); — adj. (f. -ā, or -ī) = nindak, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Deserving, or liable to, reproof, or censure, or blame; blameable, reprehensible; contemptible.
s.f. = S نندنيتو निन्दनीयत्व nindanīyatva, s.m. Blameworthiness; reprehensibleness; contemptibleness.
s.m. = S نندنيتا निन्दनीयता nindanīyatā, s.f. Blameworthiness; reprehensibleness; contemptibleness.
v.t. (dialec.) = nidarnā, q.v. v.t. (dialec.) To treat with disrespect, or contempt, &c. (i.q. nirādar karnā, q.v.).
s.f. Desire for sleep, sleepiness; drowsiness; nodding.
adj. (f. -ī), Sleepy; drowsy.
adj. (dialec.) Sleepy, drowsy (i.q. nindāsā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Sleepy; drowsy.
more: adj. (dakh.) Sleepy, drowsy ( Harris ): see ننداسا nindās-ā.

def: part. (f. -ā), Blamed, reproached, reproved; — reviled; abused; — scorned, contemned; — defamed, slandered, calumniated; — worthy of being reviled; reprehensible; odious; despicable, contemptible, low.

ety: Prk. निंदणअं; S. निन्दनीयं, rt. निन्द्
def: v.t. To blame, &c. (i.q. ninda karna, q.v.). s.f. Blame, censure, reproach, reproof; — abuse; insult; reviling; scorn; — evil-speaking, disparagement, defamation, slander; blasphemy
more: v. a. To vilify, to defame, to revile, to reproach.
subs: or nindā-lekh, s.m. Defamatory or libellous writing, a libel
(-kī), To revile; to scorn; to abuse, insult; — to speak ill (of), disparage, slander, &c.; to blaspheme.

ety: nīṅd˚ = Prk. निद्दा(इ) = S. निद्रा(ति), rt. निद्रा
def: v.n. To fall asleep; to sleep, to repose.
more: v. a. To blame, to censure.

ety: nīṅd˚, prob. = S. निन्हु(ते), rt. नि+न्हु
def: v.t. To disown, to deny.

v.t. = نندنا nindnā, 'to blame,' &c., q.v. v.t. To blame, &c. (i.q. ninda karna, q.v.). s.f. Blame, censure, reproach, reproof; — abuse; insult; reviling; scorn; — evil-speaking, disparagement, defamation, slander; blasphemy
subs: or nindā-lekh, s.m. Defamatory or libellous writing, a libel
(-kī), To revile; to scorn; to abuse, insult; — to speak ill (of), disparage, slander, &c.; to blaspheme.

def: s.f. corr. 1˚ of nindā, q.v.; — 2˚ of nidrā, q.v.
s.m. A sleeper.
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be censured or reproached; blamable; reprehensible; infamous; despicable; bad, vile (i.q. nindanīya, q.v.).
s.f. = S ننديتو निन्द्यत्व nindyatva, s.m. Blamableness; reprehensibleness; contemptibleness; infamy.
s.m. = S ننديتا निन्द्यता nindyatā, s.f. Blamableness; reprehensibleness; contemptibleness; infamy.
v.t. To swallow, &c. ( = nigalnā, q.v.). v.t. To swallow, swallow up; to gulp down; to devour; — (met.) to embezzle
v.t. To cause to swallow, &c.
more: (caus. of ننگلنا ningalnā ) v. a. (dakh.) To cause to swallow.

def: adj. S. नवनवति Ninetynine.
adj. = ninnānwe, or ninānwe, q.v.

ety: S. निपः, rt. पा with नि
def: s.m. A water-jar; — the Kadamb tree, Nauclea cadamba (i.q. nīp).
s.m. The tree Nauclea cadamba, or N. orientalis; — the plant Ixora bandhuca; — a species of Aśoka.
s.m. Drinking, imbibing; — a reservoir (of water), drinking-place, trough; a place or trough near a well for watering cattle; a well, a pool; a milk-pail.

s.m. Falling down, falling, coming down, descending, alighting; falling upon, attacking; — casting, hurling; — death, dying; — accidental occurrence or mention; — putting down as irregular or exceptional; irregular form, irregularity, exception; — the opposite extremity, the lower end; — (in Gram.) a particle, an indeclinable word (an adverb, a conjunction, an interjection).
adv. Completely, thoroughly, quite, very, extremely, exceedingly, excessively.

ety: perf. part. of nipātnā, and = S. निपातितः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Beaten down, overthrown, destroyed, killed.

s.m. Causing to fall or descend, throwing down; causing the downfall or overthrow (of), overthrowing, beating, knocking down, killing; flying down, hurrying down, swooping down (upon); — (in Gram.) putting down as an irregularity; an irregularity, an exception.
s.m. Falling down, falling; descending, alighting; flying down, flying.
s.m. Reading, study (of the sacred books), recitation; public perusal of popular poems; lecturing.
s.m. = nipāṭh, q.v. s.m. Reading, study (of the sacred books), recitation; public perusal of popular poems; lecturing.

adv. corr. of nipaṭ, q.v. s.m. Falling down, falling, coming down, descending, alighting; falling upon, attacking; — casting, hurling; — death, dying; — accidental occurrence or mention; — putting down as irregular or exceptional; irregular form, irregularity, exception; — the opposite extremity, the lower end; — (in Gram.) a particle, an indeclinable word (an adverb, a conjunction, an interjection).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Falling down, flying down or on, alighting; striking down, destroying; — an overthrower, a destroyer.
part. (f. -ā), Fallen down, fallen; descended, alighted; overthrown.

ety: nipāt˚ = S. निपातय(ति), caus. or rt. नि+पत्
def: v.t. To throw or cast down; to fell, to knock down, beat down; to overthrow, kill, destroy.
v.t. (rustic or dialec.) = nipṭānā, q.v. v.t. To complete, finish, conclude, terminate, end; to decide, settle, adjust, compose.

ety: nipaṭ˚ = Prk. निप्पट्ट(इ), or निप्पट्टे(इ), fr. S. निष्पत्ति, rt. निस्+पद्
def: v.n. To be completed, or finished; to be concluded, or terminated; to terminate, to end; to be accomplished; to be settled, or adjusted, or decided; — to be spent, or consumed, or exhausted; — to be clear, or rid (of, -se), to have done (with).
more: v. n. To terminate, to be finished.

def: v.t. To show (the teeth); to grin; to snarl; to grind or gnash (the teeth).

v. n. 1. To show one's teeth; to grin. Jo torī so morī, tū kyoṅ dāṅt nipoṛī? Prov. What you have I have. why do you grin? (Nakedness is no shame). ☞ An Englishman having expostulated with some Assamese women for bathing naked in the open they replied 'What have we that your mother and sister have not?' Tribes that were notoriously chaste while they were naked are now said to have become unchaste since they were clothed by benevolent purists. 2. To grind, or gnash one's teeth.

ety: caus. of nipaṭnā; — nipṭā˚ = niptāw = Prk. निप्पट्टआवे(इ) = S. निष्पत्(ति)+आपय (caus. aug.)
def: v.t. To complete, finish, conclude, terminate, end; to decide, settle, adjust, compose.
more: v. a. To settle, to decide, to conclude, to terminate.

def: s.m. Determination, settlement, termination, finishing, &c. (i.q. nibṭerā, nibṭāʼo, nibeṛā, qq.v.).
s.m. Accomplisher, performer, finisher, &c. (i.q. niberū, q.v.). adj. & s.m. Accomplishing, performing, finishing, &c.; — accomplisher, performer, finisher; adjuster, decider, &c.
s.m. Termination, finishing, concluding; settlement, &c. (i.q. nibṭāʼo; nibṭerā, nibeṛā; nipṭārā).
adj. (f. -ā), Clever, adroit, expert, skilful, accomplished, conversant (with), experienced (in), distinguished, eminent.
more: adj. Skilful, clever, expert, conversant, eminent.

def: Cleverness, adroitness, skilfulness, dexterity; conversancy; eminence.
Cleverness, adroitness, skilfulness, dexterity; conversancy; eminence.
Cleverness, adroitness, skilfulness, dexterity; conversancy; eminence.
s.m. Penis, membrum virile.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having no son, sonless, childless; — a childless man.
more: adj. Childless; having no son. (A curse). 1. Na-pūte kā ghar sūnā, mūrakh kā hirdā sūnā, daliddrī kā sab kuchh sūnā . Prov. Dull is the heart of the fool, dull is the childless home, compassed quite the poor man is in dullness' gloom. 2. Jis din tū janmā bahut kīye the dān, Āj na-pūtī kar chalā mere bāg meṅ ān! A dirge. Sāṅpā . The day you saw the light much gifts I did bestow, Me childless! why my garden decked, then leave me so? 3. Pūno kāte kaun na-pātī, māvash kāte rāṅḍ napūtī . Wom. song. What wretch would at the full moon spin? a widowed wretch only on the last day of the dark fortnight would spin. 4. Nīpūtṛī ke mūṅh dekhale sāt upās . Bhoj. Prov. To see a barren woman's face is to go seven days without food.

def: s.f. A childless or barren woman.
s.f. A childless or barren woman.
s.f. A childless or barren woman.
s.m. = نيڐ nīḍ, q.v. s.f. Sleep (i.q. nīṅd, q.v.). s.f. Sleep
subs: or nīṅd ućāṭ honā, v.n. Sleep to be dissipated, or to vanish; to have one's sleep broken; to be unable to sleep
(-ko), 'Sleep to visit the eyes,' to feel sleepy
adj. Having had abundance of sleep
or nīṅd-bhar-ke sonā, v.n. To sleep soundly; to have one's fill of sleep; to be at ease

def: prep. or prefix = nis (q.v.), for which it is euphonically substituted before vowels and soft consonants.
more: A negative prefix. nir-upāë ; A. lā-ilāj , adj. Without remedy; irretrievable. nir-aprādh, nir-aprādhī , adj. Innocent; guiltless. nir-uttar , n. m. Unanswerable; nihil dicit ; making no defence. nir-ādar , n. m. Disrespect. nir-ādar , adj. Held cheap; disgraced. nir-ādar k. , v. a. To insult; disgrace. nir-ādhār , adj. Without support; unsupported. nir-adhkār , adj. Powerless; without authority. nir-ās, nir-āsā , adj. Hopeless; despondent. 1. De-ke ās kar dīno nir-ās . With hope first fed, then hopeless made. 2. Jīte āsā, mue nirāsā . Prov. Hope in life, in death despir. 3. Āsā mare, nir-āsā jīye . Prov. The hopeful die, the hopeless live. nir-ās k. , v. a. To divest or deprive of hope. nir-ās h. , v. n. To be deprived, hopeless, despondent, etc. nir-antar , adj. Without interval; never-ceasing. nir-ankār , n. m. The formless, an epithet of Deity. nir-ankas , adj. Uncontrolled; unbridle…
prep. Out, without (on the outside), without (not having). The reverse of नि . As, निर्गंध nir-gandh, Scentless.

ety: Ap. Prk. णिरउ or निरउ; S. नितराम्, fr. नि+तर, compar. aff.
def: adj. (f. -ī), & adv. Sheer, downright, thorough, out and out; absolute, mere, pure, simple; single; only; unalloyed, unadulterated; — thoroughly, completely, wholly, altogether, absolutely; merely, simply
more: adj. Cont. of na aur no other. 1. Pure; clear (انميل 1). 2. Single; simple; mere. 3. Arrant; downright ( pūrā , 5). Nirā ullū hai. nirā , adv. Wholly; simply; altogether. nirī jachī biddyā . ( Mech ) Pure science. nirī chāl ( Phys .) Simple force or motion.
(see نرالا nirālā ) adj. Mere, pure, only unalloyed, single, simple.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = nīr, q.v. prep. or prefix = nis (q.v.), for which it is euphonically substituted before vowels and soft consonants.
s.m. Water; sap, juice; liquor
subs: vulg. nīraj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Water-born, produced in water; being, or living, in water; water-, aquatic; — any aquatic creature; an otter; — a lotus (in general); the water-lily; — a species of Costus, Costus speciosus

def: adj. Without desire (for); indifferent (to).
adj. Aimless, purposeless; meaningless, senseless.
s.m. See s.v. nīr, 'water.' prep. or prefix = nis (q.v.), for which it is euphonically substituted before vowels and soft consonants.
adj. (f. -ā), Without respect, unrespected, disrespected, despised, dishonoured, disgraced; — showing no respect, disrespectful; unceremonious, impolite; — s.m. Disrespect; contempt, scorn; insult
more: adj. Without respect; of bad dispositions, disrespectful, unceremonious, unpolite, disrespected.
subs: adj. (in Gram.) Expressive of disrespect; denoting contempt, &c.
(-kā), To show disrespect (to), to behave rudely (to); to dishonour, disgrace; to scorn; to insult.

def: adj. Without a receptacle; without a support or prop.
adj. = H نردهکاري निरधिकारी nir-adhikārī,adj. & s.m. Without authority or power; powerless; without right or title; — one who acts without authority, or without right or title.
adj. & s.m. = S نردهکار निरधिकार nir-adhikār, adj. Without authority or power; powerless; without right or title; — one who acts without authority, or without right or title.
more: adj. Without right or title or authority.

def: adj. Unsubjected, not subject or dependent, free.
adj. Free from egoism, devoid of selfishness; — free from pride, humble, lowly.
adj. Without food, foodless, fasting, abstaining from food; having nothing to eat; — s.m. One who fasts (whether from choice or from necessity).
adj. Free from debt.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. nīr, 'water.' prep. or prefix = nis (q.v.), for which it is euphonically substituted before vowels and soft consonants.

s.m. = S نيراجنا नीराजना nī-rājanā, s.m. Lustration of arms (a military and religious ceremony held by kings or generals on the nineteenth day of the month Aśvin before taking the field; it is still observed in some Hindū states under the name of Dasaharā).

s.m. = S نيراجن नीराजन nī-rājan, s.m. Lustration of arms (a military and religious ceremony held by kings or generals on the nineteenth day of the month Aśvin before taking the field; it is still observed in some Hindū states under the name of Dasaharā).
adj. Free from dust; having no pollen (as a flower); — free from passion; — s.f. A woman who has ceased menstruating.
adj. Free from dust; having no pollen (as a flower); — free from passion; — s.f. A woman who has ceased menstruating.
adj. = nir-āyudh, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Weaponless; without armour; unarmed; disarmed; defenceless; not warlike; imbecile.
adj. Without armour, imbecile, not warlike.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Expecting nothing, wishing or wanting nothing, free from desire, not coveting anything; content; one who is free from desire, a contented person.
adj. Devoid of form; — deprived of one's natural form; formless, shapeless, deformed; — incorporeal; — disguised; — s.m. The universal Spirit, God.
more: adj. Without form or figure, incorporeal.

def: s.m. corr. of نرخ nirḵẖ, q.v. s.m. Tariff, assize, price (of provisions, &c., as fixed by the magistrates or the police), market-rate, current price
subs: s.f. Adjustment of rates and prices; — a statement or table of prices, price-list, rate
s.m. An officer who regulates the market-rates, clerk of the market
s.m. A table of current prices, a price-current

ety: nirakh˚ = Prk. निरिक्ख(इ), or निरक्ख(इ)(?); S. निरीक्ष(ते), rt. निस्+ईक्ष्
def: v.t. To look at, see, regard, observe, view; to inspect, scan, examine closely.
more: Poet. v. a. S. निरीक्षण Pr. naikkaī he sees. To look at or examine closely.
v. a. To look at, to view, to see.

def: s.m. (old H. now obsolete) Taking out, extraction, &c. (cf. nikās; nikāl).
adj. Having no latitude; — s.m. (Astron.) The place of no latitude, i.e. the terrestrial equator
subs: s.m. A first meridian (as Lankā, or Ceylon); — a place where the sun is always vertical, and the days and nights are equal; the equatorial region.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Separate, parted, apart; retired, lonely, private; distinct; different; — strange, foreign, singular, novel, uncommon, rare, extraordinary, peculiar, curious, odd
more: adj. S. निरालय 1. See انوکها (1—4). 2. Odd. 1. Ek tum hī nirāle nahīṅ ho! 2. Jis gat par unkā pāoṅ paṛā, us gat kī chāl nirālī hai, Jis majlis meṅ woh nācheṅ haiṅ woh majlis sab se khālī hai . The dancing Darvesh. Nazīr. Stamped was each step and movement with a spell his own, In every throng he danced you saw but him alone. 3. Ghar ghar-kā, aur sāth nar kā . Prov. 'Mong houses your own house, for company a man is best.
adj. 1. Apart, aloof, retired, lonely, private. 2. Pure, mere, simple, unmixed, unalloyed. 3. Rare, strange, odd, extraordinary. نرالي nirāle, or نرالي مين nirāle meṅ, Apart, in private.
subs: adv. Apart, aloof; in private, privately.

ety: prob. nirā, q.v.+Prk. ल्लओ or लओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Mere, pure, sheer, simple, &c. (i.q. nirā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), & adv. Sheer, downright, thorough, out and out; absolute, mere, pure, simple; single; only; unalloyed, unadulterated; — thoroughly, completely, wholly, altogether, absolutely; merely, simply

ety: nirālā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The state of being apart, &c.; peculiarity; strangeness, rareness, oddness, &c.
n. m. 1. Peculiarity. 2. Strangeness. nirālī chīz ; A. unsur ; S. तत्व ( Phys .) An elementary body.
adj. Not slothful or idle; active; unwearied; zealous; — nir-ālasya, s.m. Absence of sloth or indolence; activity; zeal.
adj. Not slothful or idle; active; unwearied; zealous; — nir-ālasya, s.m. Absence of sloth or indolence; activity; zeal.
s.m. The wood apple, Feronia elephantum (syn. kaith).
adj. Free from illness or disease, healthy, well, hale; — free from taint, pure; free from defect or failing; — s.m. A wild-goat; a hog, boar.
adj. Abstaining from water, not drinking; — waterless, destitute of water.
adj. (f. -ā), Fleshless, without meat or prey; — having no sensual desires; free from covetousness
subs: adj. Not feeding on meat; living without meat; — free from sensual desires.

def: adj. (f. -inī) = nir-āmish-āśī, q.v.
adj. Without foes, free from enemies; — s.m. A proper name.

ety: cf. nidānā, of which it is prob. another form
def: v.t. To clear, to w eed (a garden, &c.); to reap (a harvest).
adj. Without apertures, or interstices, or gaps; imperforate; close, uninterrupted; — without flaw or defect, faultless.
adj. Void of passion or emotion; — s.m. An epithet of the Supreme Being.
more: adj. Void of passion, an attribute of the Hin. God.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Without collyrium or ointment, unstained, untinged, unblackened; — free from falsehood, not false, artless; — s.m. An epithet of Śiva.
s.f. An artless woman; — an epithet of the goddess Durgā; — the day of full moon.
adj. & s.m. = nir-ākār, q.v. adj. Devoid of form; — deprived of one's natural form; formless, shapeless, deformed; — incorporeal; — disguised; — s.m. The universal Spirit, God.
more: i. e. निर् prep. and अंतर Form) adj. Without form (the deity), incorporeal.

def: adj. & s.m. = nir-ākār, q.v. adj. Devoid of form; — deprived of one's natural form; formless, shapeless, deformed; — incorporeal; — disguised; — s.m. The universal Spirit, God.
more: i. e. निर् prep. and अंतर Form) adj. Without form (the deity), incorporeal.

def: adj. 'Not held by a hook'; unchecked, uncontrolled, unfettered, independent, quite free; — unruly, self-willed.
adj. Having no part or fraction remaining, whole; — without a share, having no share (of).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Without a share, &c. (i.q. nir-aṅś, q.v.); — one who has no share (of), or who has been deprived of a share.
adj. Without end or limit, endless, boundless, infinite.

adj. (f. -ā), Without any intermediate space, having no interval (of space or time); immediate; — without gaps or interstices, completely filled; closely contiguous, close, compact, dense; — closely connected, continuous; uninterrupted, continual, constant; — persevering, unintermitting; — unchanging, constant, faithful, true (as a friend); — not other or different, similar, identical; — adv. Without intervening space, immediately; — without interval, uninterruptedly, constantly, incessantly, continually, always; — compactly, closely, tightly, firmly
more: adj. or adv. Without interval, incessantly, constantly.
subs: (˚ra+abh˚), s.m. Diligent and unintermitting pursuit of study, continual reading to oneself (esp. of the sacred books); private study; — diligent and uninterrupted exercise or practice.

def: part. (f. -ā), Unfavoured; unhonoured; unpitied.
adj. (in Gram.) Not marked with the nasal symbol; not nasal.
s.f. Freedom from misfortune or calamity; a prosperous condition; security; — adj. Free from misfortune or calamity; prosperous, fortunate.
s.m. Faultlessness, guiltlessness, innocence; — adj. Unoffending, guiltless, &c. (i.q. nir-aparādhī, q.v.)
subs: or niraparādh-want, adj. (f. -vatī), Faultless, innocent, &c. (i.q. nir-aparādhī).

def: s.f. = S نرپرادهتو निरपराधत्व nir-aparādhatva, s.m. Faultlessness, innocence (i.q. nir-aparādh, q.v.).
s.m. = S نرپرادهتا निरपराधता nir-aparādhatā, s.f. Faultlessness, innocence (i.q. nir-aparādh, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Free from offence, unoffending, faultless, blameless, guiltless, innocent; — an unoffending or faultless man; guiltless one, innocent one.

s.f. Absence of hope or expectation; the being without desire (for); indifference; regardlessness; disregard, heedlessness, carelessness; independence; absence of connection or concern (with), relationlessness.

adj. (f. -ā), Without desire (for); without purpose or hope, without expectation (of), not looking (for); without regard (to), regardless (of); indifferent (to); careless, negligent; independent (of), unconnected (with), having no concern (with).
s.f. = S نرپيکشتو निरपेक्षत्व nir-apekshatva, s.m. = nir-apekshā, q.v.
s.m. = S نرپيکشتا निरपेक्षता nir-apekshatā, s.f. = nir-apekshā, q.v.

s.m. Nonsense; — adj. & adv. = nir-arthak, q.v. adj. & adv. Purposeless, useless, vain, futile, unprofitable; — unmeaning, of no signification, obsolete; — meaningless, nonsensical, absurd; — negligent, careless, indolent, good-for-nothing; — to no purpose, in vain.

adj. & adv. Purposeless, useless, vain, futile, unprofitable; — unmeaning, of no signification, obsolete; — meaningless, nonsensical, absurd; — negligent, careless, indolent, good-for-nothing; — to no purpose, in vain.
more: adj. 1. Negligent, careless, indolent. 2. Obsolete, of no signification, unmeaning, insignificant.

def: adj. & adv. Purposeless, useless, vain, futile, unprofitable; — unmeaning, of no signification, obsolete; — meaningless, nonsensical, absurd; — negligent, careless, indolent, good-for-nothing; — to no purpose, in vain.
adj. Without hope, hopeless, despairing, &c. (i.q. nirāś, q.v.)
subs: (-ko), To deprive of hope, to render despondent

def: adj. Without eating, without food; destitute of food (i.q. nir-bhojan).
adj. & s.m. See نرس ni-ras.
s.m. Throwing or casting out, ejection, removal, obviation, expulsion, abandonment; dropping (a sound or letter, &c.), omission, elision; — vomiting.
adj. (f. -ā), Without hope, hopeless, despairing (of, -se); disappointed (see nirās).

adj. (f. -ā), Sapless, dry; — flavourless, tasteless, insipid, vapid; — wanting in charms, charmless; — unfeeling, merciless; — of inferior quality, bad; — s.m. Saplessness; want of juice, dryness; want of flavour, insipidity; — want of passion or feeling.
more: adj. Dry, tasteless, comparatively bad, inferiour.

def: adj. = nirās, q.v.; — s.f. Hopelessness, despondency, despair. s.m. Throwing or casting out, ejection, removal, obviation, expulsion, abandonment; dropping (a sound or letter, &c.), omission, elision; — vomiting.
s.f. A forlorn woman; — hopelessness, despondency, despair.
adj. (f. -ī), Flavourless, insipid, vapid; — of secondary or inferior quality, inferior, bad; adulterated, alloyed (e.g. nirsā saudā, 'an inferior article'
more: adj. nir without, ras juice. 1. Adulterated; alloyed. Nirsī chāṅdī . 2. Inferior; bad. Nirsā sauaā .

ety: S. निरासनं, rt. as with nir
def: s.m. Throwing out, casting away, &c. (i.q. nirās, q.v.); refutation, contradiction, denial; — slaughter; destruction, extermination.
s.f. = S نراشتو निराशत्व nir-āśatva, s.m. Hopelessness, despair.
s.f. = S نيرستو नीरसत्व nī-rasatva, s.m. = ni-ras, q.v.
s.m. = S نراشتا निराशता nir-āśatā, s.f. Hopelessness, despair.
s.m. = S نيرستا नीरसता nī-rasatā, s.f. = ni-ras, q.v.
s.f. Hopelessness, despair (i.q. nirāsā).
s.f. See نراشي nirāsī.
adj. Having no wishes or hopes; — without a blessing, unblessed.
adj. (f. -ā), Having no support, unsupported; without shelter or refuge, destitute.

part. (f. -ā), Cast out, ejected, expelled; driven away, banished; — cast off, cast away or aside, discarded, rejected, repudiated, abandoned, deserted; disallowed; — thrown off (as from a horse); thrown (as a dart), discharged or shot off (as an arrow, &c.).
adj. Without arms, weaponless, unarmed.
part. (f. -ā), Engaged, or interested (in); attached (to), devoted (to); pleased, delighted; — rested, reposed; ceased; desisted (from).
more: n. m. S. नृत्य 1. (Sant. ene ) Dance ( māch ). 2. Gesticulation ( bhāo , 5). nirat k. , v. a. 1. To dance. 2. To gesticulate ( bhāo batānā ).

def: adj. Fruitless, profitless, vain.

adj. corr. of nir-arth, q.v. s.m. Nonsense; — adj. & adv. = nir-arthak, q.v. adj. & adv. Purposeless, useless, vain, futile, unprofitable; — unmeaning, of no signification, obsolete; — meaningless, nonsensical, absurd; — negligent, careless, indolent, good-for-nothing; — to no purpose, in vain.
adj. Without a medicine, without a remedy, incurable.
adj. Not causing trouble; not requiring effort; not fatiguing; easily attainable, easy.
adj. (f. -ā), Weaponless; without armour; unarmed; disarmed; defenceless; not warlike; imbecile.
s.m. = nir-vād, q.v. s.m. Obloquy, censure, blame, reproach; — rumour, report; — asseveration, affirmation; decision of a controversy; — absence of dispute or railing.
s.m. nir-vāh, and nibāh, qq.v.; — (dialec.) past conj. part. of nirbāhnā, q.v.

v.t. (dialec.) = nibāhnā, q.v. v.t. To carry on or through, to conduct, manage, &c. (see nibāh, and nibāh karnā); — to take care of, to protect, guard; to support, maintain; — v. int. To keep faith (with); to deal (with), to act or behave (towards); — to afford; — to wear, last, endure.

v.n. (dialec.) = nibahnā, or nibhnā, q.v. v.t. To carry on or through, to conduct, manage, &c. (see nibāh, and nibāh karnā); — to take care of, to protect, guard; to support, maintain; — v. int. To keep faith (with); to deal (with), to act or behave (towards); — to afford; — to wear, last, endure.
adj. Speechless, silent, mute, dumb.
s.m. Absence of enmity; peaceableness; — adj. Without enmity; peaceable, friendly, living in peace.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Without strength, powerless, impotent, weak, feeble; — a weak or feeble man.
s.f. A weak woman.
s.f. Powerlessness, impotence, weakness, prostration of strength.
s.f. = S نربلتو निर्बलत्व nir-balatva, s.m. = nir-balāʼī, q.v.
s.m. = S نربلتا निर्बलता nir-balatā, s.f. = nir-balāʼī, q.v.

adj. & s.m. = nir-vān, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ā), Extinguished (as a fire, &c.); lost, disappeared, extinct; — liberated from existence, dead, deceased; — (S. nir-vāṇam), s.m. Becoming extinguished; disappearance, extinction; dissolution, death; — final emancipation from matter and from the necessity for further transmigration, and reunion with the Supreme Spirit, eternal bliss; — (with Buddhists and Jains) absolute extinction, annihilation.

s.m. Insisting (upon); persistence, perseverance, pertinacity, intentness; determination, resolution; obstinacy; importunity, pressing or urging (anything), urgency; — restraint, control, rule; — accusing, accusation; contest, dispute; seizure; captivity, bondage
adj. Unfettered, unshackled; loosed (from an obligation, &c.); emancipated, freed; free; not bound (by).
s.f. (abst. s. fr. the preceding), The state of being unfettered, or freed, &c.; freedom; emancipation; the being loosed (from an obligation, &c.).
s.m. (abst. s. fr. the preceding), The state of being unfettered, or freed, &c.; freedom; emancipation; the being loosed (from an obligation, &c.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Unfettered, &c. (i.q. nirbandh); — one who is liberated, or freed, &c.; a freedman; one who is loosed (from an obligation).

adj. (f. -inī), Insisting (upon); persistent, pertinacious; persevering; intent (on); determined, resolute; obstinate, dogged; importunate; pressing, urgent; — persistently devoted (to spiritual things, &c.).
adj. Without relations, or kindred, friendless.
adj. (f. -ā), Without family or lineage; without issue, childless; extinct (race or family).
more: adj. 1. Childless, without offspring. 2. Extinct (as a race or family).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Without family or lineage; without issue, childless; extinct (race or family).
adj. Without power, or authority, or influence, &c.; powerless; helpless; enfeebled.
adj. = نربش nir-bas, q.v. adj. Without power, or authority, or influence, &c.; powerless; helpless; enfeebled.

adj. & adv. Excessive, exceeding, vehement, violent; ardent; deep, sound (as sleep); much; (in comp.) full (of), filled (with); — excessively, exceedingly, very much, &c.; — s.m. Intent and pertinacious pursuit or purpose; determined bent (of the mind, &c.); fulness of delight (in); complete conversancy (with); — resting (upon); anything to rest or depend upon; a prop, a fulcrum; — reliance, dependence, trust.
adj. (f. -ā), Fearless, undaunted; bold, daring; not afraid (of, -se), unapprehensive (of); — free from likelihood of danger or harm, secure.
more: adj. Fearless, free from fear, undaunted.

def: s.f. A fearless woman.
s.f. Fearlessness; freedom from apprehension, or from danger; security.
adj. Without food, destitute of food, foodless.

adj. (f. -ā), Without seed; seedless (as certain kinds of grapes, &c.); impotent; without issue, childless; extinct (as a race or family); extirpated, annihilated; — without foundation, groundless (as a statement, or report).
more: adj. 1. Without seed, childless. 2. Extinct (a race or family).

def: adj. & adv. = nir-vikalp, q.v. adj. Not admitting an alternative; recognizing no such distinction as that of subject and object; — not admitting of difference or otherness (between one's own spirit and the divine essence); — of unchanging purpose; being 'without variableness or shadow of turning' (the Deity; — syn. bhed-rahit).
adj. = nir-vikār, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Unchanged, unaltered; unchangeable, immutable, uniform.
s.f. Zedoary, Cucurma (or Amomum) zedoaria (a spicy plant, somewhat like ginger in its leaves, but of a sweet scent); — a species of grass, Kyllingia monocephala (used as an antidote).
more: n. f. S. निर्विषी Zedoary; curcumazedoaria , Roxb.
s. f. Zedoary (a spicy plant somewhat like ginger in its leaves, but of a sweet scent. Amomum zedoaria: Willd. Curcuma zedoaria: Roxb. ).

def: adj. Without knowledge, uninstructed, unlettered, ignorant; — without understanding, senseless; incapable of receiving instruction; — knowing without having been instructed or taught, possessed of intuitive knowledge.
adj. Without understanding, senseless.
adj. Destitute of reason or understanding, senseless, irrational, witless, ignorant, stupid; out of one's wits.
more: adj. Void of understanding, senseless, irrational.

def: s.f. Senselessness, folly.
adj. Not to be understood, incomprehensible.
s.m. A kind of pot-herb, Corchorus capsularis.
more: n. m. A potherb; Corchorus capsularis , Wat.
s. m. A kind of greens. (Corchorus capsularis).

def: adj. Guileless, simple.
s.m. The plant Semecarpus anacardium; — the marking-nut.
s.f. A species of creeper, Sanseviera roxburghiana, or S. zeylanica (from the fibres of which bow-strings are made).
s.m. = nirdayatā, or nirdayā, q.v. s.f. = S نرديا निर्दया nir-dayā, s.f. = S نرديتو निर्दयत्व nir-dayatva, s.m. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
s.m. A cave, a cavern (i.q. dara).
adj. = نردئي nir-daʼī, or nir-dayī, q.v.
s.f. = S نرديتا निर्दयता nir-dayatā, s.f. = S نرديتو निर्दयत्व nir-dayatva, s.m. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
s.f. = S نرديا निर्दया nir-dayā, s.f. = S نرديتو निर्दयत्व nir-dayatva, s.m. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
s.m. = S نرديا निर्दया nir-dayā, s.f. = S نرديتا निर्दयता nir-dayatā, s.f. Pitilessness, unmercifulness, hard-heartedness, severity, cruelty.
adj. Pitiless, merciless, hard-hearted, unkind, unfeeling, severe, cruel.

s.m. Pointing out, directing, ordering; direction, order, command; instruction; declaration; description, designation; depicting; detailed account, specification, special mention; — certainty, ascertainment.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Without property or wealth; moneyless; poor, needy, indigent; — a poor man, a pauper
subs: v.t. To strip of property or wealth, to reduce to poverty, to beggar.

def: s.f. = S نردهنتو निर्धनत्व nir-dhanatva, s.m. Absence of property or wealth; poverty, indigence, pauperism.
s.m. = S نردهنتا निर्धनता nir-dhanatā, s.f. Absence of property or wealth; poverty, indigence, pauperism.

s.m. Specification (of one out of many), particularizing; distinguishing the merits (of); — fixing or determining with accuracy; looking or inquiring (into); — determinedness, ascertainment (of a matter or subject); accuracy; certainty, positive assurance (of), persuasion; — a determination, resolution, positive purpose
more: s. m. Settling or fixing with accuracy, ascertainment.
subs: (-kā), To specify; to fix with accuracy, to distinguish (between), &c.

def: s.m. Ascertaining, determining, settling, fixing; ascertainment, &c. (i.q. nir-dhār); — agreement, arrangement.
part. (f. -ā), Defined, particularized; determined, ascertained; fixed, settled.
s.m. Injustice; unrighteousness; irreligion, impiety; — adj. (f. -ā), or -ī), Destitute of law or religion; unjust, unrighteous; immoral; irreligious; impious.
part. (f. -ā), Pointed out, shown; described, depicted; ordered, directed; specified, particularized; assigned; ascertained, determined.
adj. Blameless.
adj. corr. of nir-dosh, q.v. adj. Faultless; without defect or blemish, unblemished; blameless, guiltless, sinless, innocent
subs: or nir-dosh rakhnā, v.t. To prove to be guiltless or innocent, to clear from guilt, to exculpate, exonerate, absolve, acquit.

def: adj. Faultless; without defect or blemish, unblemished; blameless, guiltless, sinless, innocent
subs: or nir-dosh rakhnā, v.t. To prove to be guiltless or innocent, to clear from guilt, to exculpate, exonerate, absolve, acquit.

def: s.f. = S نردوشتو निर्दोषत्व nīr-doshatva, s.m. Faultlessness, blamelessness, innocence, &c.
s.m. = S نردوشتا निर्दोषता nir-doshatā, s.f. Faultlessness, blamelessness, innocence, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Faultless, &c. (i.q. nir-dosh, q.v.); — a blameless or guiltless man, innocent one.

adj. Free from either of two alternatives, indifferent in regard to opposite pairs of feelings (as pleasure and pain), neither glad nor sorry, &c.; not standing in mutual relation (to), not dependent (upon another), independent; — not striving together; free from envy or jealousy; — not causing disputes, uncontested, undisputed; — not double; not acknowledging two principles; — adv. Without quarrel or dispute, peaceably, &c.
adj. (in Gram.) Without reph, q.v.
s.m. Going out, going forth, setting out, going away, departure; receding, vanishiug; — (nir-gam) exit, issue, outlet; a door.
s.m. Going out, going forth, setting out, going away, departure; receding, vanishiug; — (nir-gam) exit, issue, outlet; a door.
adj. (f. -ā), Void of smell or scent, scentless, inodorous, unfragrant
subs: s.f. The silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum.

def: s.f. Absence of smell, want of fragrance, scentlessness.
part. (f. -ā), Gone out, gone forth, issued; come forth, come out, appeared; gone away, departed; disappeared, vanished; extinct.
s.m. A collection or list of words; a vocabulary; a table of contents, an index.
s.m. A vocabulary, &c. (i.q. nir-ghaṇṭ); — singling out the words of a sentence and writing them down in their independent form.
adj. Not odious, not detestable; — pitiless, merciless, cruel; — without shame, shameless; — detestable, abominable.
more: adj. 1. Not odious, not detestable. 2. Abominable.

def: s.f. Pitilessness, unmercifuluess, hardheartedness, cruelty.

adj. (f. -ā), Devoid of all qualities or properties; without attributes; — devoid of good qualities or virtues; unskilful; worthless, bad, vicious; — without a string, stringless, unstrung (as a necklace, &c.); — s.m. The Supreme Being (as without qualities of any kind).
more: adj. 1. Without passions or human qualities (an epithet of God). 2. Without estimable qualities, worthless, unskilful. 3. Unstrung (as, a necklace).

def: s.f. = S نرگنيو निर्गुणत्व nir-guṇatva, s.m. Absence of qualities, want of properties; freedom from all qualities (as an attribute of the Supreme Being); — absence or want of good qualities, worthlessness, wickedness, viciousness, baseness.

s.m. = S نرگنتا निर्गुणता nir-guṇatā, s.f. Absence of qualities, want of properties; freedom from all qualities (as an attribute of the Supreme Being); — absence or want of good qualities, worthlessness, wickedness, viciousness, baseness.
s.m. One who believes the Supreme Being to be void of all qualities; a believer in one Supreme Spirit without qualities of any kind.

ety: for nir-guṇṭī = S. निर्गुण्टी or निर्गुण्टिका
def: s.f. The shrub Vitex negundo; — the root of a lotus.
adj. Without laughter, or averse to laughter; grave; dull.
s.f. = nir-īkshā, q.v. s.f. Looking at, regarding, seeing; consideration; — hope, expectation.
s.f. = nir-īkshā, q.v. s.f. Looking at, regarding, seeing; consideration; — hope, expectation.
s.f. = S نريہتو निरीहत्व nir-īhatva, s.m. Indifference; absence of desire or effect.
s.m. = S نريہتا निरीहता nir-īhatā, s.f. Indifference; absence of desire or effect.

ety: nirīkh˚ or nirekh˚ = Prk. निरिक्ख(इ) = S. निरीक्ष(ते), rt. ईक्ष् with निस्
def: v.t. & n. To look at or towards, to behold, gaze at, regard, observe, view; — to look out, to spy; — to consider, inspect; — to expect, hope for (i.q. nirakhnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. To behold, to spy, to look out.

def: s.f. Looking at, regarding, seeing; consideration; — hope, expectation.
adj. = nir-īkshī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
adj. = nir-īkshī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
s.m. A look; looking at, regarding, seeing; — expecting, hope; — aspect (of the planets).
adj. = nir-īksha-mān, q.v. adj. = nir-īkshī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
adj. (f. -inī), Seeing, looking, regarding, viewing; — expecting, hoping.
part. (f. -ā), Looked at, beheld; considered; — expected, hoped.
adj. (f. -ā), Not having the use of the limbs; mutilated, maimed.
adj. Without owner or proprietor; without a master, or ruler.
s.m. The body of a plough (without the pole and ploughshare).
s.m. The body of a plough (without the pole and ploughshare).
adj. (f. -ā), Without water, waterless, dry, desert; — not mixed with water (as milk, &c.); — s.m. A desert, waste; — a fast in which water even is not to be drunk
subs: (˚la+ek), s.f. Name of the eleventh day in the light half of the month Jeṭh (May-June), on which even the drinking of water is forbidden; the fast of that day
To go without food and water, to keep a rigid fast.

def: s.m. A religious penance or fast in which water is not drunk the whole day (see nirjalaikādaśī, s.v. nirjal).
adj. Unpeopled, uninhabited, unfrequented; depopulated, deserted, lonely, desolate.
more: adj. Without a person or individual, desert, uninhabited.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Not becoming old; undecaying, imperishable, immortal; — s.m. (S. nir-jaram) Ambrosia, the food of the gods.
